title: Resource implementation guidance normalizes resource IDs through typed parsers instead of preserving Azure-returned casing

provenance.originSummary: Synthetic benchmark scenario designed to exercise the rule directly.
provenance.whyItMattered: Use this case to keep the resource-implementation skill forward-looking on resource ID normalization, even when older provider code still contains mixed-case or raw-string patterns.
provenance.genericCondition: Synthetic implementation scenario focused on forward-looking resource ID normalization guidance rather than legacy provider drift.

description: A synthetic implementation-guidance scenario where a contributor compares raw resource ID strings and writes an Azure-returned nested resource ID directly into Terraform state. The correct guidance should treat read-side ID handling as case-insensitive through the shared typed parser and should persist the parser's canonical `.ID()` form instead of preserving arbitrary casing returned by the RP.

scope.notes: Synthetic implementation scenario focused on forward-looking resource ID normalization guidance rather than legacy provider drift.

mustCatch.description: The guidance should say import, read, refresh, or migration paths must parse resource IDs through the shared typed parser instead of relying on raw string equality against Azure-returned IDs.
The guidance should say provider-managed IDs written back to Terraform state must use the parser's canonical `.ID()` form rather than preserving arbitrary casing from API responses.

mustNotFlag.description: The guidance must not treat the Azure-returned resource ID string as the value to preserve in state when a typed parser exists for that ID shape.
The guidance must not approve raw string comparison as the normal read-side strategy for matching or normalizing resource IDs.

mustHaveSections: minimal set of code changes needed

notes: Use this case to keep the resource-implementation skill forward-looking on resource ID normalization, even when older provider code still contains mixed-case or raw-string patterns.
